ops.title: Superset.app's pty-daemon runtime updater writes into the app bundle and once clobbered host-migrations, killing the host-service; diagnosis paths and fix
ops.body:
Superset (superset.sh desktop, yasyf-home Mac) installs its pty-daemon runtime (`resources/bin|lib|share`, standalone node + node-pty) INTO the app bundle at `/Applications/Superset.app/Contents/Resources/resources/` at runtime. On 2026-07-12 that mechanism wiped `resources/host-migrations/`, so host-service died at startup ("Can't find meta/_journal.json", exit 1) and the app reported "host not running".

Fix that worked: fresh install from the GitHub release (superset-sh/superset, `Superset-<ver>-arm64-mac.zip`), then restore `resources/lib` + `resources/share` from the old bundle so the long-running pty-daemon (which holds live sessions and execs node-pty's `spawn-helper` from that path per spawn) keeps working — otherwise new sessions fail with `posix_spawnp failed`.

Diagnosis paths: `~/Library/Logs/Superset/main.log` (host-service exit lines), `~/.superset/host/<org-id>/host-service.log` and `pty-daemon.log`, `~/.superset/bin/superset` CLI (`status`, `terminals create` to test spawn end-to-end). `codesign --verify --deep /Applications/Superset.app` detects bundle tampering.
